And then Ogilvy said, if you can't advertise yourself, what hope do you have of being able to advertise anything else? And pretty good point, right? But what you need to get really firmly in your head is that these people, as they transcend a certain income level where there is rational relationship between the goods or services and the income. You transcend that if you're going to in earned income knock down a million, two million, three million a year because pretty much there's almost nothing you can provide and provide enough of with rational relationship between goods and services and prices and fees to get to that income level. Now it doesn't mean you can't justify it and it doesn't mean you can't make it have satisfactory perceived value. We'll talk about that. But really, pretty much at that price fee level, there's always some place they could go to get truth be told a very comparable good or service for less money. So it transcends the rational connection. And so yesterday, a guy I had a consulting day with almost a year ago, Services, and of course I gave him a contract and quoted him a fee then for his project that I did with him a year ago. And so he's now ready to do something so he's back. And it's $110,000 fee plus points on the gross. And I graciously let last year's fee stay in place, which I thought was extremely generous on my part. Now truth be told, there's at least 10 copywriters who could do what I'm good at do for him. Even if I arrogantly think and would tell you that I might be a little better than them and the result may be a little better, he could probably spend $35,000 and get perfectly satisfactory, profitable, extraordinarily profitable results. So why 110? Because at $35,000, I can't make a million dollars a year from copywriting and only devote 20% of my time to it. It's that simple. It has nothing to do with any rational connection to the product or the service. It has to do with how many jobs I want to do. That's what it has to do with. And then the question is how do you find enough people who want to do that? His willingness to do it has nothing to do with the copy. It has to do with all sorts of other things that we're going to talk about. So this disconnect in your own head of product, service, deliverable, price, fee really has to occur, has a clean break that one has nothing whatsoever to do with the other and a big key to it is what business are you in and ultimately you probably need to be in the business of whatever your name is. So let's talk about the key strategies to all this. Some we're just going to skim over. Some we're going to get to as we go along down some depth. So one is self-appointment. So self-appointment simply means you're not waiting around for anybody to give you a diploma or certify you or authorize you or appoint you or whatever to do what it is that you do or to charge what it is that you wish to charge. So any sort of formal process that may exist for certifications and graduations and bestowing
